Although Google’s TPUs have posed the biggest challenge to Nvidia’s AI processor dominance so far, the rivalry is still a largely amicable one.

headline: SK Hynix quarterly profit jumps five-fold on strong AI chip demand
summary: STORY: :: SK HynixSouth Korea's SK Hynix on Thursday posted a record quarterly profit, and forecast AI chip demand would exceed manufacturing capacity.The Nvidia supplier reported an operating profit of just over $25 billion for January-March.That's a five-fold jump from last year, and in line with analyst estimates.Shares in the firm rose over 2% in morning trade, but later fell back into negative territory. The stock has jumped nearly 90% so far this year.SK Hynix said that client requests for chips over the next three years already exceed production capacity.The outlook signals strong momentum for the high bandwidth memory chips used in AI, likely keeping supplies constrained and prices high.SK Hynix said earlier it was accelerating capacity expansion.That includes bringing forward the opening of a chip plant in South Korea to meet surging demand.It sees limited impact from the war in the Middle East, having taken steps to secure inventory, diversify suppliers and lock in long-term energy contracts.
